prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: TaraHome blog cover, landscape 3:2 / 1536x1024 crop-safe
Primary request: Create a unique topic-specific cover image for an article titled "Why Is Your Home Assistant Database So Big?" about Home Assistant recorder storage, SQLite history, safe cleanup, and local control.
Scene/backdrop: warm residential utility shelf or small home office corner with a compact local smart-home hub/mini PC, SSD or backup drive, a small Home Assistant-style device nearby without logos, organized Ethernet/power cables, and subtle dark-green status lights. It should feel like a real homeowner's local automation setup, not a data center.
Subject: real local hub hardware and storage device as the visual focus; a nearby smart-home sensor or switch can hint that this is a Home Assistant house.
Style/medium: clean photographic/editorial smart-home still life, premium practical technology photography, realistic materials and shadows.
Composition/framing: landscape cover, useful at small card size, subject centered with clean negative space around edges for cropping; no inset frames.
Lighting/mood: warm natural window light, trustworthy, calm, tidy, locally controlled.
Color palette: warm residential neutrals, dark green local-control accents, small touches of muted blue/gray hardware; avoid purple and abstract gradients.
Text: no text.
Constraints: no embedded headline, no title poster, no readable UI, no logos, no brand names, no charts, no graphs, no diagrams, no floating UI cards, no translucent panels, no inset frames, no progress bars, no decorative routing/network/path line overlays, no people, no hands, no messy cable tangle, no stock-photo blur, no fake dashboard screen.
